assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Extended channel provides 'up to 24 months of support' vs 'standard 14 months' and includes a comparison table showing 14 months for Regular vs up to 24 months for Extended

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Additional cost' during extended period (months 15-24) and explains costs in detail in the 'Cost Implications' section

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: The comparison table shows 'Same as Regular during standard support period' for version availability, and the response explains Extended keeps versions available for 24 months vs 14

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Security debt risk: If you delay minor upgrades too long, you accumulate security patches and deprecated API changes' in the considerations section

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response includes pre-migration checks with commands to verify version availability and specifically mentions 'Verify 1.31 is available in Extended'

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Extended compliance window: 24-month support helps with slow enterprise change cycles' and identifies Extended as ideal for maximum control environments

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response states 'Delayed EoS enforcement: No forced upgrades until month 24' and warns about 'EoS enforcement' at the end of extended support period

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Minor version auto-upgrades: No (manual only, except at end of extended support)' and emphasizes 'Manual minor upgrade responsibility' throughout
